title: Pooling Suite: Browser-Based Meta-Analysis Engine with Ten Heterogeneity Estimators
author: Mahmood Ahmad
body: How do methodological choices among heterogeneity estimators and confidence interval methods affect pooled meta-analytic estimates in practice? Two built-in datasets, BCG vaccine (13 studies) and magnesium for myocardial infarction (8 studies), were analyzed across all estimator and CI method combinations. Pooling Suite, a browser-based application of 1,840 lines, implements ten tau-squared estimators and three CI methods, producing a 30-cell comparison table with forest plots, Baujat plots, GOSH analysis, and full influence diagnostics including Cook distance. The BCG dataset showed pooled log-RR ranging from -0.71 (95% CI -1.06 to -0.37) under ML to -0.74 under Hedges, with tau-squared varying from 0.30 to 0.51. Leave-one-out analysis identified the Madras and Chingleput studies as dominant heterogeneity sources across all ten estimators. This tool provides the first side-by-side browser-based comparison of ten meta-analytic estimators, validated against R metafor with 25 Selenium tests. A limitation is that the current version does not implement profile likelihood or Kenward-Roger corrections for small meta-analyses.

sentences.text: How do methodological choices among heterogeneity estimators and confidence interval methods affect pooled meta-analytic estimates in practice?
Two built-in datasets, BCG vaccine (13 studies) and magnesium for myocardial infarction (8 studies), were analyzed across all estimator and CI method combinations.

Pooling Suite, a browser-based application of 1,840 lines, implements ten tau-squared estimators and three CI methods, producing a 30-cell comparison table with forest plots, Baujat plots, GOSH analysis, and full influence diagnostics including Cook distance.
The BCG dataset showed pooled log-RR ranging from -0.71 (95% CI -1.06 to -0.37) under ML to -0.74 under Hedges, with tau-squared varying from 0.30 to 0.51.
Leave-one-out analysis identified the Madras and Chingleput studies as dominant heterogeneity sources across all ten estimators.

checks.name: single paragraph
checks.detail: Body must not contain blank-line paragraph breaks.

checks.name: no headings
checks.detail: No markdown headings allowed in body.

checks.name: no links
checks.detail: No links or DOI links allowed in body.

checks.name: result sentence has interval
checks.detail: Sentence 4 should include a confidence interval.

checks.detail: Sentence 4 should name an effect measure or test metric.
